title: Pallenis aurea
intro: Pallenis aurea, also known as the Golden Snakeroot, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Pallenis aurea include sesquiterpene lactones, such as 8-dehydro-19-hydroxy-isostepholidone, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Pallenis aurea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
